items.title: langchain-ai/langgraph shows active graph orchestration development
items.summary: LangGraph emphasizes explicit state graphs, durable execution patterns, checkpointing, and multi-agent workflows. Recent issue themes center on deployment ergonomics and graph state debugging.

items.title: openai/openai-agents-python focuses on provider-native agent primitives
items.summary: OpenAI Agents SDK positions handoffs, tools, tracing, guardrails, and model-provider integration as first-class primitives. Recent examples focus on production tracing and multi-agent handoffs.

items.title: Developers compare graph-based agents with provider-native SDKs
items.summary: Discussion favors LangGraph when workflows need explicit state machines and independent model providers. OpenAI Agents SDK is viewed as simpler when teams already standardize on OpenAI APIs and tracing.

items.title: Agent builders report debugging pain around long-running workflows
items.summary: Practitioners praise explicit graph execution for debuggability but warn that state management and retries become complex. Provider-native SDKs are praised for fast starts and criticized for lock-in risk.

items.title: langgraph package shows frequent releases
items.summary: Package release cadence indicates active maintenance. API churn risk remains relevant for production teams that need stable graph definitions.

items.title: openai-agents package emphasizes trace and guardrail ergonomics
items.summary: Package metadata and examples highlight tracing, guardrails, tools, and handoffs. The dependency surface is smaller than full graph-framework stacks.

items.title: LangGraph docs emphasize checkpointing and explicit control flow
items.summary: Documentation is strongest for teams that need human-in-the-loop checkpoints, resumable workflows, and graph-level control over state transitions.

items.title: OpenAI Agents docs emphasize handoffs, guardrails, and tracing
items.summary: Documentation is strongest for teams that want direct provider integration, built-in tracing, and guardrail primitives with less orchestration boilerplate.

items.title: Agent framework adoption requires tool permission and trace review
items.summary: Risk review highlights prompt injection, unsafe tool use, missing approval gates, and insufficient replay traces as the main production blockers independent of framework choice.
